tip: Define one primary success metric for the campaign before you open it, like quality of hire at 90 days, not just speed.
why_it_works: Single-metric clarity prevents the team optimising for the wrong outcome and aligns hiring manager and recruiter.
evidence: 89% of organisations rank quality of hire as critical, only 25% feel confident measuring it

tip: Run a 30 minute intake with the hiring manager before writing the ad, and capture must-haves, scorecard and target start date.
why_it_works: Structured intake makes teams three times more likely to cut time-to-hire and twice as likely to improve quality of hire.
evidence: Misalignment forces 41% more mid-search requisition changes and adds 38% to time-to-fill

tip: Cap each recruiter at five to seven active roles, then add a sixth only if you cut a stale one.
why_it_works: Workload above this range correlates with sharply lower close rates in mid-market hiring teams.
evidence: Drop from 7 to 5.8 reqs per recruiter lifted close rate from 42% to 79% in two quarters

tip: Pick four to six channels for the campaign, not all of them. Too many channels create noise and dilute spend.
why_it_works: Most successful recruiters use 4 to 6 active channels matched to role type and seniority.
evidence: 4 to 6 channels is the sweet spot across sourcing studies

tip: Set a 48 hour service-level for first response to every applicant before the campaign goes live.
why_it_works: Only 19% of candidates hear back inside 24 hours today, so a 48 hour standard is a real differentiator.
evidence: 3 to 5 day average first contact lag drives ghosting

tip: Plan the AU campaign sprint to fall in late January to March or mid-July to September when job-search traffic peaks.
why_it_works: SEEK traffic spikes after summer and at the start of the second half, lifting application volumes.
evidence: Seasonal SEEK job-ad and search patterns

tip: Check the internal team before posting externally and ask if anyone could move sideways or up into the role.
why_it_works: Internal moves fill in 10 to 15 days vs 42 days for external hires and improve retention.
evidence: Companies with strong internal mobility see 2x increase in retention

tip: Decide on one or two concrete proof points for the EVP before writing copy, then anchor every channel to them.
why_it_works: Activated EVPs lift engagement by 23% and cut turnover by 24%.
evidence: Deloitte EVP measurement data

tip: Write the rejection email and the offer email on day one so the team is not improvising under pressure later.
why_it_works: Prepared touchpoints lift candidate NPS and prevent ghosting at decision time.
evidence: 61% of job seekers have been ghosted after an interview

tip: Set a stop date for the campaign, not just a start date. Roles that drift past 60 days lose hiring manager attention.
why_it_works: Aged requisitions distort capacity planning and erode candidate quality.
evidence: Stage-aging is a top dashboard signal

tip: Publish one short employee video per campaign showing a real day in the role, filmed on a phone, not a production set.
why_it_works: Employee-shared content gets eight times the engagement of brand-channel content.
evidence: 8x engagement uplift for employee posts

tip: Name the hiring manager in the ad and link to their LinkedIn. Faceless ads convert worse than ones with a person attached.
why_it_works: Personal attribution builds trust and lifts apply rates, especially with Gen Z.
evidence: Gen Z candidates trust employee voice over brand voice

tip: Cut buzzwords like world-class, dynamic and rockstar from the ad. Concrete nouns and verbs outperform vague positives.
why_it_works: Generic praise reads as low-signal and reduces perceived credibility.
evidence: 53% of hires report ads differed significantly from reality

tip: Pick two EVP themes and repeat them everywhere, like flexibility and growth, rather than listing ten.
why_it_works: Repetition across surfaces is what makes an EVP land internally and externally.
evidence: 75% of HR leaders find internal EVP comms challenging

tip: Ask three current employees for one sentence each on why they stay. Use those quotes in the ad and on the careers page.
why_it_works: First-person staff quotes outperform generic culture copy and read as authentic.
evidence: Employee voice drives trust in candidate decision making

tip: Show the team. Use a photo of the actual team the hire will work with, not stock photography.
why_it_works: Real photos outperform stock on every recruitment marketing benchmark for trust and click-through.
evidence: Authentic visuals lift career-site conversion

tip: Add one video testimonial on the careers page from someone in a similar role.
why_it_works: Video on a landing page can lift conversion by up to 80%.
evidence: On-page video conversion boost

tip: Address the workload question in the ad. If the role is busy, say so honestly and explain the support around it.
why_it_works: Excessive workload is the top reason AU employees leave, so candidates screen for it.
evidence: 26% of AU employee departures cite excessive workload

tip: Mention flexibility specifics, not just the word flexible. Spell out the hybrid pattern, hours and trust model.
why_it_works: 65% of companies highlight flexibility as a core EVP element, so specifics are the differentiator.
evidence: Flexibility specificity drives candidate interest

tip: Run a single recurring employee content series instead of a one-off campaign film.
why_it_works: Consistent micro-content compounds; one-off films decay within weeks.
evidence: Short-form video outperforms longer formats for completion and shares

tip: Keep the job description between 200 and 400 words. Apply rates peak in this range.
why_it_works: Apply rates run 8 to 8.5% in this band and drop below 5% for ads over 700 words.
evidence: Appcast analysis of 10.2 billion clicks

tip: Use a short, searchable job title between one and four words. Avoid clever titles like Coding Ninja.
why_it_works: Titles of one to three words generate more clicks; long titles rank poorly in search.
evidence: 1-3 word titles average 3.41 clicks vs 2.75 for 13+ words

tip: Lead the ad with the outcome the person will own in the first 90 days, not with company history.
why_it_works: Outcome-led copy converts better and signals you have a clear plan for the role.
evidence: Candidates rate concrete role detail above company puffery

tip: List four to six must-have requirements only. Move the rest to a clearly labelled nice-to-have list.
why_it_works: Clear must-have framing lifts application rates from under-represented candidates.
evidence: Clear must-have labelling shifts gender ratio of applicants

tip: Show the salary band in the ad. Candidates self-select faster and trust the employer more.
why_it_works: Candidates rank pay among the most important pieces of ad information; visible pay reduces wasted applications.
evidence: Almost one in four job seekers say pay is the most important ad detail

tip: Replace masculine-coded words like competitive, dominate and aggressive with synonymous neutral words.
why_it_works: Removing gender-coded terms increases applications from women without losing male applicants.
evidence: 29 to 40% application uplift across studies

tip: Drop degree requirements unless the role legally needs one. Replace with the underlying skills.
why_it_works: Skills-first job ads expand candidate pools by up to six times.
evidence: Skills-based hiring expands pools 6x overall

tip: Include three concrete benefits that matter to the role, not a list of every perk.
why_it_works: Specific, relevant benefits outperform exhaustive perk lists for click-to-apply rates.
evidence: Targeted benefits framing lifts ad performance

tip: Use one screening question in the ad to weed out clearly unqualified applicants without adding friction.
why_it_works: A single targeted screener lifts hire likelihood without hurting completion.
evidence: Screener questions can lift hire likelihood by 50%

tip: Write the ad in second person, talking to the candidate, not in third person about the role.
why_it_works: Second-person ads convert better because they feel like a conversation, not a brochure.
evidence: Direct candidate-facing copy lifts apply rates

tip: Add one sentence on growth or learning. Career progression beats free coffee for most candidates.
why_it_works: Professional development is highly valued by both Millennials and Gen Z.
evidence: Gen Z prioritise purpose and growth over traditional perks

tip: Open with a hook sentence that names the problem the role exists to solve.
why_it_works: Problem-led openings outperform generic job-summary openings on click-to-apply.
evidence: Outcome-led copy lifts engagement

tip: Use the same job title across the ad, the careers page and the ATS posting. Inconsistency tanks search ranking.
why_it_works: Consistent titles improve search visibility on Indeed, SEEK and Google for Jobs.
evidence: Consistent titles outrank creative aliases

tip: Use plain English at year-9 reading level. Long, jargon-heavy sentences lose candidates fast.
why_it_works: Plain language widens the applicant pool and improves comprehension on mobile.
evidence: Plain language consistently lifts conversion in copy testing

tip: Test two title variants in parallel for the first 48 hours, then keep the winner.
why_it_works: Job title is the single highest-leverage A/B test on apply rate.
evidence: Title length and wording shift click-to-apply rates substantially

tip: Mention location specifically. Vague location terms like nationwide or flexible reduce apply rates.
why_it_works: Candidates filter heavily on location, so specificity drives qualified clicks.
evidence: Location specificity is a top job board filter

tip: Add a one-line note inviting candidates to apply even if they meet most but not all requirements.
why_it_works: Encouragement statements lift under-represented applications without lowering quality.
evidence: Inclusive framing widens applicant pool

tip: Lead the AU campaign on SEEK for volume, then layer LinkedIn for specialist roles and Indeed AU as a second tier.
why_it_works: SEEK is the gravitational centre of AU job search; specialist roles benefit from LinkedIn targeting.
evidence: SEEK applications per ad at record highs in August 2025

tip: Ask your team for two referrals each in the first week. Most referrals happen during work hours.
why_it_works: Referred candidates convert at ~30% vs ~7% for other applicants and retain 25% better at one year.
evidence: Referrals deliver higher conversion and retention

tip: Set a flat referral bonus that is meaningful to your business. Anywhere from a thousand to five thousand dollars is the typical band.
why_it_works: Clear, fair bonuses drive participation without distorting decisions.
evidence: Typical referral bonuses range 1k to 5k per successful hire

tip: Run silver-medalist re-engagement at the start of any campaign. Past finalists convert at 50% or higher.
why_it_works: Silver medalists already know your business and convert at high rates with low effort.
evidence: Silver-medalist re-engagement consistently exceeds 50% response

tip: Personalise outreach by mentioning a specific project or post by the candidate. Boilerplate InMail loses to specifics.
why_it_works: Personalised outreach lifts InMail response from 5-10% to 25-35%.
evidence: Personalisation triples cold outreach response rates

tip: Keep InMail under 400 characters. Short, specific messages outperform long pitches.
why_it_works: InMails under 400 characters get 22% higher response rates.
evidence: Short-form outreach data from LinkedIn benchmarks

tip: Send sourcing outreach Tuesday to Thursday, 8 to 10am or 4 to 6pm local time.
why_it_works: Mid-week, edges of the workday consistently outperform Monday mornings and Friday afternoons.
evidence: Outreach timing benchmarks

tip: Use a three-touch outreach sequence across LinkedIn, email and phone within 10 to 14 days.
why_it_works: Multi-channel sequences triple reply rates over single-channel sends.
evidence: Multi-channel sequences achieve 15 to 25% reply vs 5 to 10% single-channel

tip: Run a small programmatic test once you have data on which job boards convert. Reallocate spend in real time.
why_it_works: Programmatic ad buying outperforms manual board spend on targeting and effectiveness.
evidence: Programmatic shows 51% better targeting and 48% better effectiveness

tip: Build a talent pool for every role you have hired more than once, then nurture it monthly.
why_it_works: Talent pools shorten time-to-fill on the next opening and lower cost-per-hire.
evidence: Monthly nurture cadence is the recommended baseline

tip: Segment the talent pool by skill, location and engagement before sending any nurture email.
why_it_works: Segmentation lifts open and reply rates and feels less spammy.
evidence: Granular segmentation is the strongest predictor of nurture engagement

tip: For AU skilled trade roles, advertise in trade-specific channels alongside SEEK because broad boards underperform.
why_it_works: Trade and care occupations remain in long-term AU shortage with low generalist response.
evidence: 139 AU occupations on shortage list since 2021, heavy in trades and care

tip: Post the ad on Indeed Apply if available. Simplified mobile apply lifts completed applications five times.
why_it_works: Indeed Apply delivers 5x more completed applications than non-Apply postings.
evidence: Indeed Apply benchmark

tip: Run a single TikTok or Reels short for Gen Z roles. Day-in-the-life beats slick recruiter pitches.
why_it_works: Gen Z spends hours daily on short-form video and trusts employee voice over brand voice.
evidence: Short-form video receives up to 35% higher engagement than static posts

tip: Make sharing the role a one-tap action for staff. Pre-write the LinkedIn copy they can paste.
why_it_works: Employee-shared posts outperform brand-channel posts on engagement and trust.
evidence: Employee-shared content gets 8x engagement

tip: Acknowledge every application within 48 hours, even with an automated message that sets expectations.
why_it_works: Fast acknowledgement reduces ghosting and lifts candidate NPS.
evidence: Only 19% of candidates hear back inside 24 hours today

tip: Tell candidates the next step and the expected timeline in the acknowledgement email.
why_it_works: Clear timelines reduce anxiety and the urge to accept competing offers.
evidence: 42% of candidates want stronger recruiter communication

tip: Use SMS for time-sensitive updates like interview confirmations. Texts open in three minutes.
why_it_works: SMS gets a 98% open rate and a 45% response rate, with a median read time of three minutes.
evidence: SMS open and reply rates dwarf email

tip: Ask candidates for their preferred contact channel at the apply stage. Text, email or phone.
why_it_works: Channel preference respect lifts engagement and reduces no-shows.
evidence: Candidates getting texts rate experience 50% higher

tip: Always send a rejection within five days of the interview. Silent rejections drive negative reviews.
why_it_works: 72% of job seekers share negative experiences online, hurting future campaigns.
evidence: 55% of job seekers avoid companies after reading negative reviews

tip: Personalise rejection emails for interviewed candidates with one specific comment from the panel.
why_it_works: Personal rejections protect employer brand and keep silver medalists warm.
evidence: Candidate resentment is at record levels

tip: Run a candidate NPS survey after every campaign closes. Aim for a score above thirty.
why_it_works: Above zero is acceptable; 30 to 70 is strong; over 70 is elite.
evidence: Standard CNPS benchmark interpretation

tip: Ask one open question in the NPS survey: what would have made this better. Use the answers in the next intake.
why_it_works: Open feedback surfaces fixable friction points the recruiter cannot see.
evidence: Only 41% of companies currently ask for feedback

tip: Cap the interview process at four stages for non-executive roles. More stages predict drop-off.
why_it_works: Excessive interview rounds increase abandonment and counter-offer risk.
evidence: 31% of candidates wait 2-3 weeks for first interview, driving disengagement

tip: Share the interview format and questions in advance. Surprise interviews favour confident extroverts, not the best fit.
why_it_works: Preparation increases signal in interviews and improves candidate experience.
evidence: Structured interviews improve quality of hire

tip: Confirm the interviewer name and seniority in the calendar invite. Candidates prep better when they know who they will meet.
why_it_works: Clear context reduces no-shows and improves answers.
evidence: Candidate communication consistently ranks as top improvement area

tip: Make the verbal offer within 48 hours of the final interview. Delay is the largest cause of declined offers.
why_it_works: Speed prevents counter-offers and competing offers from landing first.
evidence: Time-to-hire up 24% since 2021, driving acceptance decline

tip: Ask the candidate during the offer call whether their current employer is likely to counter-offer.
why_it_works: Pre-processing the counter-offer question reduces last-minute withdrawals.
evidence: Counter-offers account for ~10% of declines

tip: Send the written offer the same day as the verbal. A 24 hour gap is enough to lose the candidate.
why_it_works: Documented offers tighten commitment and reduce reneging.
evidence: Offer process speed predicts acceptance

tip: Use a structured scorecard with weighted criteria for every interview. Unscripted interviews favour the wrong candidates.
why_it_works: Structured scoring is the single highest-impact lift to interview-to-offer conversion.
evidence: Structured interviews improve quality of hire and reduce bias

tip: Train every hiring manager on the scorecard before they interview. Only 38% have any interview training.
why_it_works: Even one hour of training measurably improves consistency and reduces bias.
evidence: 38% of hiring managers receive formal interview training

tip: Test for skills before reviewing CVs for high-volume roles. Skills tests predict performance better than resumes.
why_it_works: 96% of employers using skills tests before CV review report higher-quality hires.
evidence: Skills-first hiring delivers 50% lower time-to-hire and 89% better retention

tip: Keep work samples short. Twenty minutes maximum for a take-home. Long tasks drive away strong passive candidates.
why_it_works: Excessive work samples are a top reason strong candidates withdraw.
evidence: Candidate experience research links task length to drop-off

tip: Use the same questions across all candidates for the same role. Comparable answers are the only path to fair scoring.
why_it_works: Consistent questions reduce bias and improve hiring manager confidence.
evidence: Structured interviews improve calibration and outcomes

tip: Limit panel size to three. Larger panels slow decisions without improving signal.
why_it_works: Panel-size discipline keeps decision cycles fast.
evidence: Stage and panel count drive decision lag

tip: Score independently first, then debrief. Anchoring to the loudest voice corrupts the consensus.
why_it_works: Independent scoring before debrief is a standard structured-interview safeguard.
evidence: Independent scoring reduces anchoring bias

tip: Use a behavioural question for each must-have skill. Past behaviour predicts future performance.
why_it_works: Behavioural questions outperform hypothetical and trick questions on signal.
evidence: Structured behavioural interviews are the highest-validity interview type

tip: Reject CVs only against documented criteria. Gut-feel screening reproduces past bias.
why_it_works: Criterion-based screening keeps the funnel defensible and consistent.
evidence: AI and human screening both reproduce historical bias without criteria discipline

tip: Use AI to draft job ads and screening questions, then have a human review every line before it goes live.
why_it_works: AI saves recruiters one workday a week but should not replace judgement on the final output.
evidence: 74% of TA pros say AI makes hiring more efficient

tip: Disclose when AI is used in screening and give candidates an opt-out path.
why_it_works: Transparency is now an expected practice and lifts trust scores.
evidence: Gartner recommends opt-out paths for AI screening

tip: Audit AI screening output quarterly for demographic skew. If older or female candidates are screened out at higher rates, retrain or override.
why_it_works: AI screeners reproduce historical bias without active monitoring.
evidence: Stanford 2025 study shows AI screeners favour older male candidates

tip: Use a chatbot for FAQ, scheduling and basic eligibility checks. Hand off to a human as soon as the conversation gets complex.
why_it_works: Chatbot handoff design protects candidate experience while saving recruiter time.
evidence: 38% of recruitment processes now use chatbots

tip: Run async video pre-screens with three to five short questions, not ten. Long async screens hurt candidate experience.
why_it_works: Concise async pre-screens cut screening time by 80% without overwhelming candidates.
evidence: Async video pre-screens cut screening time by 80%

tip: Let candidates retake async video answers at least once. Single-take recording penalises nerves, not performance.
why_it_works: Retake permission improves the fit between recording and real performance.
evidence: Async video can feel impersonal and intimidating without affordances

tip: Automate interview scheduling. Reclaim recruiter hours and remove the slowest part of the calendar dance.
why_it_works: Scheduling automation saves recruiters 15 plus hours a week.
evidence: Async video and scheduling tools save 15+ hours per recruiter weekly

tip: Pick one AI workflow to roll out per quarter, not five at once. Adoption fails when teams try too many things.
why_it_works: Focused rollout outperforms scattered AI pilots in TA adoption studies.
evidence: Bersin TA Revolution recommends sequenced AI rollout

tip: Never let AI make the final hiring decision. Use it for triage and ranking, with humans deciding.
why_it_works: AI plus diverse human review outperforms either alone and protects against bias.
evidence: Best practice is AI-augmented, human-decided screening

tip: Set a 5-day target from application to first interview offer for high-volume roles.
why_it_works: Most candidates withdraw if they wait two to three weeks for the first interview.
evidence: 31% of candidates wait 2-3 weeks for first interview today

tip: Block interview slots in the calendar for the next two weeks before the ad goes live.
why_it_works: Pre-blocked interview slots remove the largest source of scheduling delay.
evidence: Scheduling lag is a top driver of time-to-hire

tip: Combine the first interview and the work sample where possible. Two-stage processes beat four-stage on acceptance.
why_it_works: Fewer stages tighten the funnel without losing signal when each stage is well-designed.
evidence: Time-to-hire correlates with offer acceptance

tip: Track time-in-stage. The slowest stage is the bottleneck and where to spend your fix-it hour.
why_it_works: Time-in-stage is the single most actionable speed metric.
evidence: Stage-aging is the top dashboard signal across recruiting tools

tip: Aim for time-to-hire under 32 days for AU non-executive roles. The AU median is 32 days, 16% faster than global.
why_it_works: AU has a structural speed advantage; matching the median is a credible baseline.
evidence: Median time-to-hire 32 days in AU

tip: Use one shared inbox or ATS thread per candidate. Email tennis loses candidates.
why_it_works: Single-threaded comms reduces dropped balls and improves response times.
evidence: Candidate comms ranks as top experience improvement area

tip: Run interviews same-week, not over a fortnight. Compressed cycles improve calibration and decision quality.
why_it_works: Compressed cycles keep the panel aligned and the candidate engaged.
evidence: Stage-aging predicts both acceptance and quality

tip: Pre-approve the salary band with finance before posting. Late approval kills offers.
why_it_works: Salary delay is a top decline cause.
evidence: Compensation accounts for ~40% of offer declines

tip: Pre-draft the offer letter and the rejection email. Day-of writing slows decisions.
why_it_works: Templated docs let recruiters move on offer day in minutes, not hours.
evidence: Offer speed predicts acceptance

tip: Post the role on LinkedIn from the hiring manager's profile, not just the company page. Personal posts outperform brand posts.
why_it_works: Employee-led posts get more reach and trust than company-page posts.
evidence: Employee-shared content has 8x engagement

tip: Run one piece of social content per week for the duration of the campaign, not all at launch.
why_it_works: Consistent cadence beats a single launch blast for sustained applications.
evidence: Recurring content compounds across campaign window

tip: Use vertical video under 60 seconds for Reels, Shorts and TikTok. Horizontal repurposes badly.
why_it_works: Short-form vertical video outperforms longer formats for completion and shares.
evidence: Under-60-second video wins on completion

tip: Add captions to every video. Most viewers watch with sound off, especially on mobile.
why_it_works: Captions lift watch-time and accessibility.
evidence: Mobile-default sound-off behaviour drives caption ROI

tip: Repurpose every interview, panel and team photo across channels for a month. One asset, many surfaces.
why_it_works: Repurposing stretches limited content budget and reinforces brand recall.
evidence: Consistent multi-surface presence lifts employer brand recall

tip: Pin the role at the top of the company LinkedIn page until it closes. Pin replacements are free reach.
why_it_works: Pinned posts capture every profile visit during the campaign.
evidence: LinkedIn page optimisation tactic

tip: Pair every social post with a direct apply link, not a careers-page link. Every extra click loses candidates.
why_it_works: Reducing clicks from interest to apply lifts conversion.
evidence: Mobile drop-off rises with every click

tip: Tag every silver-medalist candidate in the ATS with the role, the score and a re-engage date.
why_it_works: Silver-medalist re-engagement is the fastest, cheapest fill on the next role.
evidence: Silver-medalist re-engagement converts at 50%+

tip: Send a quarterly update to your talent pool with one role, one story and one company milestone.
why_it_works: Light, regular touches keep the pool warm without overwhelming.
evidence: Monthly to quarterly cadence is the recommended baseline

tip: Ask candidates if they want to join the talent pool when they decline an offer or are rejected.
why_it_works: Explicit opt-in talent pools are higher-quality and lower legal risk than silent retention.
evidence: Pool consent improves engagement

tip: Build a small talent pool of internal employees who could be promoted. Internal hires fill in 10 to 15 days.
why_it_works: Internal talent visibility shortens fills and lifts retention.
evidence: Internal hires fill in 10 to 15 days vs 42 days external

tip: Personalise nurture emails with the candidate's name, the role they applied for and a relevant update.
why_it_works: Personalisation is the strongest predictor of nurture engagement.
evidence: Personalised nurture lifts open and reply rates

tip: Tag candidates by skill, not by job title. Skills tags age better and surface candidates for adjacent roles.
why_it_works: Skills-based tagging widens future re-use of the pool.
evidence: Skills-based matching lifts pool reach by up to 6x

tip: Pick five KPIs for the campaign and review them every week. More than five becomes background noise.
why_it_works: Focused dashboards drive weekly action; wide dashboards drive contemplation.
evidence: Three to five KPIs reviewed weekly is the recommended cadence

tip: Track time-to-fill, time-to-hire, applicant-to-interview, interview-to-offer and offer acceptance every week.
why_it_works: These five metrics cover the full funnel and surface every meaningful bottleneck.
evidence: Standard recruitment dashboard composition

tip: Use a 42 day time-to-fill global benchmark or 32 days for AU. Above those, investigate.
why_it_works: Comparison to benchmarks turns numbers into decisions.
evidence: SHRM 42 day average and SmartRecruiters AU 32 day median

tip: Track cost per hire. Average non-executive cost per hire is around $5,500 USD.
why_it_works: CPH anchors channel ROI decisions and budget conversations.
evidence: SHRM 2025 non-executive CPH $5,475

tip: Score quality of hire at 90 days using a one-question manager rating: would you hire them again.
why_it_works: A single repeated question is more reliable than a complex framework for small teams.
evidence: 66% of TA pros use manager ratings for QoH

tip: Combine 90-day manager rating with 12-month retention to get a usable quality-of-hire signal.
why_it_works: Pre-hire and post-hire signals together beat either alone.
evidence: Two-category quality-of-hire framework

tip: Ask applicants where they first heard about the role, in their own words. Open-text answers surface real influence.
why_it_works: Last-touch source-of-hire misses the channel that actually influenced the candidate.
evidence: Source-of-hire reflects application channel, not influence

tip: Compare channel cost per quality hire, not cost per application. Cheap clicks can be expensive hires.
why_it_works: Quality-weighted channel cost is the only honest spend metric.
evidence: Quality-weighted attribution outperforms volume-weighted

tip: Aim for a healthy interview-to-offer rate between 25% and 38%. Below 15% means calibration or quality issues.
why_it_works: Bench mark anchors expose either intake or interview-design problems.
evidence: Industry average 15 to 25%, top performers 28 to 38%

tip: Aim for offer acceptance above 85%. Below 70% signals speed or pay issues.
why_it_works: Acceptance rate is the cleanest decision-stage signal.
evidence: Healthy 85 to 90%, poor under 70%

tip: Measure candidate NPS after every campaign. Above zero is acceptable, 30 plus is the goal.
why_it_works: CNPS surfaces friction the team cannot see internally.
evidence: Standard NPS interpretation

tip: After the campaign, write a short retrospective with one thing to keep and one to change for next time.
why_it_works: Continuous improvement compounds across campaigns; ad hoc reflection does not.
evidence: Cadence-based review is the recommended dashboard discipline

tip: Track applicant-to-interview rate. The industry average is around 2%; under 1% means top-of-funnel problems.
why_it_works: Top-of-funnel ratio diagnoses ad and channel problems early.
evidence: 2% applicant-to-interview industry average

tip: Watch first-week application volume as the leading indicator. Slow starts almost never recover.
why_it_works: Early signal lets you fix the ad or channel mix in week one, not week four.
evidence: Week-one volume predicts campaign outcome

tip: Confirm interview slot acceptance by SMS within 30 minutes. Texts close the loop where email does not.
why_it_works: Most texts are read within three minutes; same-day confirmation reduces no-shows.
evidence: SMS 98% open rate, 3-minute read time

tip: Run a five-minute pre-interview call to set context. Cold interviews waste both sides' time.
why_it_works: Light pre-interview contact lifts interview-to-offer conversion.
evidence: Recruiter touch predicts candidate engagement

tip: Tell the candidate verbally before sending the rejection email. For finalists, do not let the email be the news.
why_it_works: Verbal first-rejection protects employer brand and silver-medalist warmth.
evidence: Candidate resentment is at record levels in 2024

tip: Confirm the start date in writing with a welcome note from the hiring manager within 24 hours of acceptance.
why_it_works: Quick post-acceptance touchpoints reduce renege risk in the gap before start.
evidence: Offer-to-start gap is a known reneging window

tip: Keep the application form under five minutes, including resume upload. Apply rate quadruples vs 15-minute forms.
why_it_works: Forms under 5 minutes convert at 12.5% vs 3.6% for forms over 15 minutes.
evidence: Appcast and HirezApp form length benchmarks

tip: Cut the form to ten required fields or fewer. Every extra field cuts completion by 10 to 15%.
why_it_works: Field-count discipline is the highest-leverage apply-flow optimisation.
evidence: 4-to-3-field reduction lifts conversion 50%, 11-to-4 lifts 120%

tip: Test the application on a phone before launching. Mobile drop-off is 30 to 50% higher on non-mobile-first sites.
why_it_works: 67% of applications are submitted on mobile; phone-first testing prevents leaks.
evidence: Mobile applicants drop off 30-50% more on non-mobile-first apply flows

tip: Allow resume upload by file or autofill from LinkedIn. Manual re-typing is the biggest mobile drop-off trigger.
why_it_works: Easy file paths protect mobile completion rates.
evidence: Mobile applicants take 80% longer per field

tip: Show a progress bar on multi-step applications. Knowing they are nearly done lifts completion materially.
why_it_works: Progress indicators reduce abandonment on longer forms.
evidence: 3 to 4 step forms with progress indicators outperform

tip: Do not ask for the same data twice. Auto-fill name and email from the resume parse.
why_it_works: Duplicate fields are a top mid-application drop-off trigger.
evidence: Form duplication kills completion

tip: Decide whether the role is volume or specialist before choosing channels. Volume needs SEEK and Indeed; specialist needs LinkedIn and direct sourcing.
why_it_works: Channel mix should match role shape, not recruiter preference.
evidence: LinkedIn 25% interview-to-offer for specialist; Indeed 76% hires for volume

tip: For AU healthcare, education, construction, engineering or science roles, plan a longer campaign and a smaller channel mix.
why_it_works: These are the five sectors with persistent AU shortages.
evidence: Most affected AU sectors per JSA 2025

tip: Brief the team on overseas candidates if local pipelines are thin. Six in ten AU employers now use overseas talent.
why_it_works: AU SMEs need to plan for this realistically rather than assume local pipelines.
evidence: 60% of AU employers use overseas talent for shortages

tip: After three campaigns, compare your benchmarks against AU norms: 32 days time-to-hire, 65 applicants per hire.
why_it_works: Benchmark comparison surfaces where the team is structurally above or below market.
evidence: AU benchmarks per SmartRecruiters 2025

tip: Show one piece of evidence for every EVP claim. If you say flexibility, show the rota; if growth, show a promotion path.
why_it_works: Evidence-backed claims outperform abstract promises and survive candidate due diligence.
evidence: 53% of candidates report ads differing from reality

tip: Audit your Glassdoor, SEEK Company Reviews and Google reviews quarterly. Candidates check before applying.
why_it_works: 55% of job seekers avoid companies after reading negative reviews.
evidence: Candidate review-checking behaviour

tip: Respond to every recent negative review with a calm, specific reply. Silence reads as confirmation.
why_it_works: Owner responses materially lift candidate trust scores.
evidence: Review responsiveness drives perceived employer credibility

tip: Run a quick LinkedIn search of past employees who left in good standing. Boomerang hires are fast and high-quality.
why_it_works: Returning employees onboard faster and bring intact knowledge.
evidence: Passive sourcing studies favour warm networks

tip: Source from communities and meet-ups before job boards for niche technical roles. Domain trust converts.
why_it_works: Warm community outreach converts at 40 to 60%, well above cold sourcing.
evidence: Warm outreach reply rates 40-60%

tip: Track which channel produced each shortlist, not just each hire. Channel-to-shortlist ratio is a cleaner quality signal.
why_it_works: Hire-only attribution misses the channels that consistently produce strong finalists.
evidence: Multi-touchpoint attribution beats last-touch

tip: Use AI to draft three candidate persona briefs before the campaign. Faster than starting from blank, sharper than recycled.
why_it_works: AI-drafted briefs save hours and force the team to pick the persona that matters.
evidence: AI saves recruiters ~20% of time

tip: Have AI summarise candidate experience survey free-text into the top three themes after each campaign.
why_it_works: AI summarisation makes qualitative data actionable on small teams.
evidence: AI augments analysis tasks effectively

tip: Track diversity of the applicant pool, the shortlist and the hire separately. Drop-off between them surfaces structural bias.
why_it_works: Stage-level diversity tracking pinpoints where the funnel narrows unfairly.
evidence: Stage-level monitoring is the recommended bias-audit approach

tip: Review hiring manager satisfaction quarterly. Their honest feedback predicts campaign quality.
why_it_works: HM satisfaction is one of the three most-used QoH inputs.
evidence: 44% of TA pros use HM satisfaction for QoH

tip: Capture cost per quality hire, not just cost per hire. Divide spend by hires that pass the 90-day rating.
why_it_works: Quality-adjusted CPH reveals where spend is wasted on hires that do not stick.
evidence: Two-category quality framework

tip: Make the apply button visible above the fold on every device. Hidden CTAs lose mobile candidates.
why_it_works: Above-the-fold CTAs lift conversion on small screens.
evidence: Mobile-first design lifts completion

tip: Avoid forcing candidates to create an account before applying. Account walls drop completion by 30% or more.
why_it_works: Frictionless apply is the single biggest lever on completion.
evidence: Account creation is a top abandonment trigger

tip: Refresh the job ad if it has been live more than 14 days without conversion. Stale ads keep underperforming.
why_it_works: Two-week refresh cycle catches dead campaigns before budget is wasted.
evidence: Recurring ad performance optimisation tactic

tip: Run one campaign at a time per recruiter on volume roles. Splitting attention slows every fill.
why_it_works: Sequenced campaigns close faster and produce better hires than parallel ones.
evidence: Capacity research shows reqs-per-recruiter discipline

tip: Run a weekly 20 minute campaign stand-up with the hiring manager. No agenda beyond pipeline and decisions.
why_it_works: Short, regular touchpoints keep decisions moving and the panel calibrated.
evidence: Cadence-based reviews accelerate hiring
